I mean 9000 bucks It's a winner okay if I put in 1500 bucks I get 200 clicks and I'm only getting you know my EPC is only you know buck 50 now It's a loser. I start looking at the traditional CPA networks This is really interesting This is Russell going off on a tangent, but if you guys pay attention this is worth like tens of millions of dollars So hopefully you guys will get the gist so this is this is kind of what happens So in a traditional CPA offer someone's gonna an affiliate's gonna drive traffic They come to a page right here on this page to ask for name email phone number Address and on a page number two they're gonna come back here and ask for a credit card number And over here there's gonna be an upsell and over here. They're gonna call someone on the phone and sell them coaching right? So that's a traditional traditional model for a you see the whole model is it getting cut off My zoomed out all the way. Oh there we go that better Okay, so the affiliate comes in the drive into a short landing page asking for name address phone number after they submit that the next page Then ask for credit card information and after that there's an upsell and then they call them on the phone They sell them coaching that's the traditional CPA model. So what's happening is this dude back here that owns the phone center Okay, he comes back here. He finds his fill it says hey if you generate lead right here. I anyone that gives me their credit card I will pay you Like crazy numbers like 40 bucks so 40 bucks or 60 bucks for every person that gives me their credit card Okay, the reason why so I'm gonna come on the phone over here And I'm gonna make $250 and so I'm gonna risk the money if you generate the lead for me But I'll pay 60 bucks for everyone who purchases this free this free thing. Okay, so that's traditionally how CPA network works Now this person right here this phone center person They're trying to make back their money as quick as they can right because they just spent 60 bucks right here And they got zero dollars in return They got an upsell and they might make $20 here then come on the phone make $250 here But they're trying to figure out how else can I make money from this data? Okay, we call these people very affectionately We call them data horse. That's who they are what they do. Anyone know The title of the book was supposed to be pigheaded discipline Okay, and if you notice through the book all through the book He says pighead discipline over and over and over again because that was the initial title of the book But the publishers made him change it to the ultimate sales machine But he he comes back he talks about like to do this game to win this game It's all about pigheaded discipline So he went after the top 100 companies and fortune 500 and every single week He'd mail them out a package or something something lumpy some lumpy mail in the mail Then two weeks later you call him on the phone and say hey Did you get the thing I sent you the big lumpy mail and he fall up on the phone Then two weeks later you send out another something in the mail then two weeks later follow up And consistently for month in month out year after year He would he would contact people twice per month once or something in the mail once or something on the phone And I remember the exact stats the numbers but when all said done I think he landed like 60 or so of the of the fortune 500 companies hired him to be their be their guy Okay, because he was had pigheaded discipline was consistent. He kept doing it kept doing it and kept doing it Okay, it always amazed me always people that want me to promote their products yet their pigheaded discipline is not there They'll call my office or even my office to call my office and that's it it stops right there They're not going to print out nothing. I did one time I've actually done this twice so far I couldn't get someone to return my call or whatever and I got really kind of frustrated I'm like I got to get this person to call me back And so I found the pizza hut that was right by their office And I ordered a pizza and I paid the pizza person extra five dollars to write a note on the box And the pizza man comes and delivers the pizza the person's office and both times I did I got a call back Okay, because it's different like someone delivers you a pizza You're going to find out who that person is Um, so it's all about like just being different and being able to to stick out there But also being aggressive with it not just giving up after the first try or the second time I feel bad. We were annoying But if you're if you annoy someone and they don't do a deal with you, guess what? Okay. He's like no, we only publish like really big people who bubble bubble I'm just like Just kind of really rub me wrong like if you did any research ahead of time like you know like these are all my peers These are all my friends. He's like sure So he sent an email to his list and he made uh, I don't know 10 sales for my product I sent an email to my little list. Because an affiliate program if you look at it, it's a glorified business opportunity Come join my affiliate program and I'm going to teach you how to make how to make money So everyone that had a big affiliate program all had some kind of info product teaching people how to run an affiliate program Okay, so every one of you guys should be in the business opportunity space with some kind of product teaching people how to be Affiliates because that's where you're going to start recruiting the creamier crop. That's what used to happen on a traditional launch So what we started doing is it's kind of like figure how can we How can we get more of this and less of this? Okay, so we started that we do initial launch like that and um And usually you get your big peak and it starts dying down and we started getting closer to the weekend We started creating these weekend contests. It just happened during the weekend So we announced them like on a friday. We say hey this weekend's coming. We know you guys are about to head home for the weekend Um, what we're gonna do is we're gonna be giving away Whatever it is like we got 12 different stereo systems So anyone who can make least x amount of cells is going to get a stereo or um, rind I stood this with uh with uh their facebook ads It was right when the ipad came out brand new and I wasn't even planning on promoting it And also and I got an email on a friday night right before I was about to leave the office and said anyone gets 100 cells This weekend gets a free ipad. All right, so One of the coolest things that I did it was the most effective was first thing you had mentioned earlier that I'd done That's where you go in and you Buy a product or coaching now. This is if you're going to fill it for somebody else. It's not necessarily your own product, but you go and You do the jay bram model of give give give before you ask And so like I caught somebody in a product launch and I'd been to jeff walkers product launch manager training and so forth so Uh, the guy I'd actually paid money for and I was doing, you know, he was private coaching with me They're they're caught short in this launch So I just volunteered completely. I said, you know, you guys are stuck. I don't need any commission. I don't need any money Anything just let me help you, you know, I'm receiving value from your coaching. You've not paid for it but I gave And for two weeks I acted like I was going to make 20 on the deal. I had no commitment at all That has led to four other deals for me That's turned into six figure business just because of that one relationship. Those guys saw me They it was a it was a you know partnership in that launch. They had three partners in launch And every one of them because I act like their employee I gave them my very best stuff Didn't charge them a penny didn't take anything out of the launch and that's just led to all kinds of opportunities That's awesome.
